---
title: "NYC Flights Data"
author: "Mia Fitzgerald"
date: "4/16/2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = TRUE, message = TRUE)
library(knitr)
library(tidyverse)
library(nycflights13)

```


### First 6 Origin/Destination Pairs


```{r}
OD_flights <- flights %>%
  count(origin, dest)

OD_flights %>%
  head(6) %>%
  kable()

```


^[Question 1 complete]


### Using airports, retrieve just the latitude and longitude of the origin airports and add those columns
to your frequency table. Rename latitude and longitude to reflect these are the origin’s coordinates.
Relocate the columns to be next to the origin column. Display a table of the first 6 rows.


```{r}
airports_3 <- airports %>% 
  select(lat, lon, faa) 

OD_flights %>%
  left_join(airports_3, join_by(origin == faa)) #XLOOKUP (Dayblink)


```
